title: salix × rubens var. viridis
intro: Salix × rubens var. viridis, also known as the Green Willow, is a variety of the Crack Willow tree, which is a hybrid of two willow species. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its bark and leaves, which contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × rubens var. viridis include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and lowering fever, making it a popular remedy for treating various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
